commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: - [P1] A maintainer or contributor needs to provide current-head real behavior proof and decide the provider/runner compatibility risk; automation cannot supply the contributor's live setup proof.; Cleared: No concrete security or supply-chain defect was found; the new tool execution interpretation remains a merge-risk/product-safety decision rather than a confirmed bypass. (sha=34b20dd4c0ce5a43a8e0bb89a3d5d395c9250c17)
commands.review_summary:
The PR extracts serialized plaintext tool-call parsing, scrubbing, stream normalization, and promotion into a private `@openclaw/tool-call-repair` workspace package and wires provider plus embedded-runner paths through it.

PR surface: Source +1995, Tests +2809, Config +9, Other +2. Total +4815 across 20 files.

Reproducibility: Partly, but not as a high-confidence live reproduction in this review. The discussion includes concrete redacted Discord logs, and current-main source shows XMLish standalone calls are not promoted through the same path, but I did not run a live provider reproduction.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] A maintainer or contributor needs to provide current-head real behavior proof and decide the provider/runner compatibility risk; automation cannot supply the contributor's live setup proof.

target.title: fix: promote serialized tool calls via repair package
target.body:
## Summary

- Extracts serialized plaintext tool-call parsing, scrubbing, stream normalization, and standalone promotion into the private internal `@openclaw/tool-call-repair` workspace package.
- Keeps `src/plugin-sdk/tool-payload.ts` as the public compatibility facade while provider and embedded runner paths share one repair implementation.
- Promotes provider final messages that split serialized tool calls across adjacent text blocks, preserving exact argument bytes when valid and repairing structural block-boundary splits only at known tool-call headers/parameter openers.
- Adds provider regressions for split XML parameter calls, exact argument splits, and bracketed header/payload splits.

## Verification

- `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile`
- `pnpm test src/plugin-sdk/provider-stream-shared.test.ts src/plugin-sdk/tool-payload.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.tool-call-normalization.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose` - 103 tests passed.
- `env -u OPENCLAW_TESTBOX pnpm check:changed`
- `AUTOREVIEW_AUTO_TESTS=0 .agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local` - clean; no accepted/actionable findings.
- `git diff --check origin/pr/87093...HEAD`

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Standalone serialized plaintext tool-call output is repaired into structured `toolCall` blocks before user-visible delivery. Provider and embedded runner surfaces now share the same repair path, including adjacent text-block splits without mutating valid argument bytes.

Real environment tested: Local macOS source checkout on this PR branch after the `@openclaw/tool-call-repair` refactor. No live provider credentials or remote Testbox were required for the unit/changed-lane proof.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```sh
pnpm install --frozen-lockfile
pnpm test src/plugin-sdk/provider-stream-shared.test.ts src/plugin-sdk/tool-payload.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.tool-call-normalization.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose
env -u OPENCLAW_TESTBOX pnpm check:changed
AUTOREVIEW_AUTO_TESTS=0 .agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local
git diff --check origin/pr/87093...HEAD
```

Evidence after fix: Focused Vitest passed 2 shards covering `src/plugin-sdk/tool-payload.test.ts`, `src/plugin-sdk/provider-stream-shared.test.ts`, and `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.tool-call-normalization.test.ts`, 103 tests total. `check:changed` selected all lanes because `pnpm-lock.yaml` changed and completed shrinkwrap guards, typecheck all, lint, and import-cycle checks successfully. Autoreview finished clean with no accepted/actionable findings.

Observed result after fix: Provider and embedded runner tests promote serialized text tool calls into structured `toolCall` blocks, with regressions covering adjacent text-block splits, exact argument-boundary preservation, and bracketed header/payload boundary repair. User-visible stripping still removes oversized serialized tool-call blocks without promoting them.

What was not tested: Live gateway/provider proof was not rerun after this package refactor. The delegated Testbox `check:changed` attempt failed before code checks because the remote checkout had no merge base for `origin/main...HEAD`; the same changed check passed locally with Testbox delegation disabled.

Refs #86924
target.author: fuller-stack-dev
